title: Crispy Dutch Snap Pea Gratin
description: A peppery, gooey Dutch gratin featuring blanched sugar snap peas and a golden Dutch Gouda topping.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 C (400 F) and grease a 9x9 inch baking dish.
Blanch the sugar snap peas in boiling salted water for 2 minutes, then transfer them immediately to ice water to stop cooking, and drain well.
Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat, then whisk in the flour to create a roux and cook for 1 minute.
Slowly pour in the milk while whisking constantly until the sauce thickens to a creamy consistency.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper, then fold in the grated Gouda until melted.
Spread the blanched peas evenly in the prepared baking dish and pour the cheesy sauce over them.
Sprinkle the gluten-free breadcrumbs and sliced chives on top of the gratin.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes until the breadcrumbs are golden and the top is bubbly.
Remove from the oven, let it rest for 5 minutes, then serve warm.
